moduleDescription:
The parsecfg module implements a high performance configuration file parser. The configuration file's syntax is similar to the Windows .ini format, but much more powerful, as it is not a line based parser. String literals, raw string literals and triple quoted string literals are supported as in the Nim programming language.

Example of how a configuration file may look like:

# This is a comment.
; this too.

[Common]
cc=gcc     # '=' and ':' are the same
--foo="bar"   # '--cc' and 'cc' are the same, 'bar' and '"bar"' are the same (except for '#')
macrosym: "#"  # Note that '#' is interpreted as a comment without the quotation
--verbose

[Windows]
isConsoleApplication=False ; another comment

[Posix]
isConsoleApplication=True

key1: "in this string backslash escapes are interpreted\n"
key2: r"in this string not"
key3: """triple quotes strings
are also supported. They may span
multiple lines."""

--"long option with spaces": r"c:\myfiles\test.txt"

Here is an example of how to use the configuration file parser:

Configuration file example

charset = "utf-8"
[Package]
name = "hello"
--threads:on
[Author]
name = "nim-lang"
website = "nim-lang.org"

Creating a configuration file

Reading a configuration file

Modifying a configuration file

Deleting a section key in a configuration file

Supported INI File structure

entries.name: CfgEventKind
entries.code:
CfgEventKind = enum
  cfgEof,                   ## end of file reached
  cfgSectionStart,          ## a `[section]` has been parsed
  cfgKeyValuePair,          ## a `key=value` pair has been detected
  cfgOption,                ## a `--key=value` command line option
  cfgError                   ## an error occurred during parsing
entries.description: enumeration of all events that may occur when parsing

entries.name: CfgEvent
entries.code:
CfgEvent = object of RootObj
  case kind*: CfgEventKind   ## the kind of the event
  of cfgEof:
    nil
  of cfgSectionStart:
    section*: string         ## `section` contains the name of the
                             ## parsed section start (syntax: `[section]`)
  of cfgKeyValuePair, cfgOption:
    key*, value*: string     ## contains the (key, value) pair if an option
                             ## of the form `--key: value` or an ordinary
                             ## `key= value` pair has been parsed.
                             ## `value==""` if it was not specified in the
                             ## configuration file.
  of cfgError:              ## the parser encountered an error: `msg`
    msg*: string             ## contains the error message. No exceptions
                             ## are thrown if a parse error occurs.
entries.description: describes a parsing event

entries.name: CfgParser
entries.code: CfgParser = object of BaseLexer
entries.description: the parser object.

entries.name: open
entries.description: Initializes the parser with an input stream. Filename is only used for nice error messages. lineOffset can be used to influence the line number information in the generated error messages.

entries.name: close
entries.description: Closes the parser c and its associated input stream.

entries.name: getColumn
entries.code:
proc getColumn(c: CfgParser): int {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1", raises: [],
                                    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the current column the parser has arrived at.

entries.name: getLine
entries.code:
proc getLine(c: CfgParser): int {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1", raises: [], tags: [],
                                  forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the current line the parser has arrived at.

entries.name: getFilename
entries.code:
proc getFilename(c: CfgParser): string {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the filename of the file that the parser processes.

entries.name: errorStr
entries.code:
proc errorStr(c: CfgParser; msg: string): string {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a properly formatted error message containing current line and column information.

entries.name: warningStr
entries.code:
proc warningStr(c: CfgParser; msg: string): string {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a properly formatted warning message containing current line and column information.

entries.name: ignoreMsg
entries.code:
proc ignoreMsg(c: CfgParser; e: CfgEvent): string {.gcsafe, extern: "npc$1",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a properly formatted warning message containing that an entry is ignored.

entries.name: next
entries.description: Retrieves the first/next event. This controls the parser.

entries.name: Config
entries.code: Config = OrderedTableRef[string, OrderedTableRef[string, string]]

entries.name: newConfig
entries.code: proc newConfig(): Config {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new configuration table. Useful when wanting to create a configuration file.

entries.name: loadConfig
entries.description: Loads the specified configuration from stream into a new Config instance. filename parameter is only used for nicer error messages.

entries.name: loadConfig
entries.description: Loads the specified configuration file into a new Config instance.

entries.name: writeConfig
entries.code:
proc writeConfig(dict: Config; stream: Stream) {.raises: [IOError, OSError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Writes the contents of the table to the specified stream.
Note: Comment statement will be ignored.

entries.code:
proc `$`(dict: Config): string {.raises: [IOError, OSError],
                                 tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Writes the contents of the table to string.
Note: Comment statement will be ignored.

entries.name: writeConfig
entries.code:
proc writeConfig(dict: Config; filename: string) {.raises: [IOError, OSError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Writes the contents of the table to the specified configuration file.
Note: Comment statement will be ignored.

entries.name: getSectionValue
entries.code:
proc getSectionValue(dict: Config; section, key: string; defaultVal = ""): string {.
    raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Gets the key value of the specified Section. Returns the specified default value if the specified key does not exist.

entries.name: setSectionKey
entries.code:
proc setSectionKey(dict: var Config; section, key, value: string) {.
    raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the Key value of the specified Section.

entries.name: delSection
entries.code:
proc delSection(dict: var Config; section: string) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Deletes the specified section and all of its sub keys.

entries.name: delSectionKey
entries.code:
proc delSectionKey(dict: var Config; section, key: string) {.raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Deletes the key of the specified section.

entries.name: sections
entries.code: iterator sections(dict: Config): lent string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates through the sections in the dict.
